mechanism_narrative: Growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137928K in March 2014 showing steady recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns do not exceed 50% baseline in a t-test (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is constrained below 0.25%, the zero lower bound eliminates risk-free yield, forcing portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS < 0.25% periods fail to exceed the unconditional baseline mean with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirms broad-based demand recovery that outpaces supply constraints, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 433907 and HOUST at 907 as of early 2014
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST does not significantly predict 20-day returns (F-test p > 0.05) or the signal's hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals capacity utilization recovery before labor market tightening occurs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this effect disappears when unemployment is low.
failure_condition: During UNRATE > 6.0% periods, INDPRO growth does not exhibit a positive Rank IC (> 0.03) with 60-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) alleviates supply bottlenecks and signals near-term margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate deteriorating consumer demand that triggers downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) alongside a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) causes the market to discount aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change < -0.3 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 2.5
failure_condition: The UNRATE-CPIAUCSL interaction signal does not generate negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) concurrent with falling industrial production (INDPRO) constitutes a stagflationary monetary squeeze that compresses valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative in more than 60% of cases (binomial test p > 0.05).
